description_long: This is a partially ruined, rectangular brick house with only the exterior walls remaining and no roof or interior floors. The structure features red brick walls with several large window and door openings, some of which are missing frames or glass, giving it an abandoned appearance. Vegetation and overgrowth are visible along the base of the walls, especially at the front, suggesting long-term neglect. The front facade includes a small porch area with a partial roof supported by columns, and remnants of steps leading up to the entryway. The rear and side walls are mostly intact, with some utility elements like an air conditioning unit and a fenced area visible. The overall look is utilitarian and functional, with minimal decorative elements, typical of early-to-mid 20th-century urban residential architecture.
description: A rectangular, partially ruined brick house with missing roof, overgrown vegetation, and a small porch at the front. The structure is utilitarian, with large window and door openings and minimal decorative features.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entryway, porch with columns, and significant overgrowth along the base of the wall.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long brick wall, window openings, and the absence of a roof, with vegetation visible along one edge.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back wall, utility elements like an air conditioning unit, and a fenced area, with fewer openings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the side wall, more overgrowth, and the partial roof structure over the porch area.
attribute_entryway_design: Simple entryway with a small porch, partial roof, columns, and steps, minimal decorative elements
